chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Slanderous Accusation

text: 5063 - رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلَّذِي يَقْذِفُ اِمْرَأَتَهُ قَالَ "يُجْلَدُ" قُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ عَفَتْ عَنْهُ قَالَ "لاَ وَ لاَ كَرَامَةَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5063 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who accuses his wife (of adultery).
Imam (as) said: "He is to be flogged."
I asked: "What if she pardons him?"
Imam (as) said: "No, and no honor (for him)."
